phrases.intent_en: Ask about the astronomical clock show
phrases.text_local: À quelle heure est le spectacle de l'horloge astronomique ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kel UHR eh leh spek-TAHK duh lor-LAJ ah-stroh-noh-MEEK
phrases.cultural_note: The astronomical clock shows a new display at 12:30pm (not noon). Crowds gather early — the show lasts about 30 minutes.

responses.english: It's at twelve thirty sharp, not at noon.
responses.phonetic: say tah DOOZ ur trahn-TAH pray-SEEZ, pah ah mee-DEE
responses.french: C'est à douze heures trente précises, pas à midi.

phrases.text_local: Peut-on monter au sommet de la tour ?
phrases.pronunciation: puh-TON mon-TAY oh so-MAN duh lah TOOR
phrases.cultural_note: You can climb 332 steps to the viewing platform. The climb is steep but worth it for views across the city to Germany.

responses.english: Yes, it's three hundred thirty-two steps. The view is worth it.
responses.phonetic: wee, say trwah sahn trahnt-DUR MARSH. lah voo voh luh koo
responses.french: Oui, c'est trois cent trente-deux marches. La vue vaut le coup.

phrases.cultural_note: The cathedral is built from Vosges sandstone, which turns pink-orange at sunset. It's nicknamed 'the pink cathedral'.

responses.english: It's Vosges sandstone. It turns pink-orange at sunset.
responses.phonetic: say duh GREH day VOHZH. eel duh-vee-AN ROHZ-oh-rahn-ZHAY oh koo-SHAY duh soo-LEH
responses.french: C'est du grès des Vosges. Il devient rose-orangé au coucher du soleil.

phrases.text_local: Pourquoi la cathédrale n'a-t-elle pas de deuxième tour ?
phrases.pronunciation: poor-kwah lah kah-tay-DRAL nah-tel pah duh duh-ZYEM TOOR
phrases.cultural_note: The north spire was never built due to funds. The single spire is unique among great cathedrals — most have two.

responses.english: The second tower was never built due to lack of funds. It's unique.
responses.phonetic: lah duh-ZYEM TOOR nah zhah-MAY ay-tay kohn-stroo-EE foh-tuh duh fohn. say oo-NEEK
responses.french: La deuxième tour n'a jamais été construite faute de fonds. C'est unique.

phrases.text_local: Quelle est la hauteur de la cathédrale ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel eh lah oh-TEUR duh lah kah-tay-DRAL
phrases.cultural_note: At 142 meters, it was the world's tallest building from 1647 to 1874. It's still one of the tallest sandstone structures.

responses.english: The spire reaches 143 meters, one of Europe's tallest cathedrals.
responses.phonetic: el feh sahn kah-RAHN-dur METR duh oh
responses.french: La flèche atteint 143 mètres de hauteur, l'une des plus hautes cathédrales d'Europe.
